document.source_title: Sudan hunger crisis deepens as UN warns millions face acute food shortages
document.summary: The UN has issued a warning about a deepening hunger crisis in Sudan, with over 19.5 million people facing crisis levels of food insecurity. The conflict, which began in April 2023, has led to widespread displacement and economic collapse, severely disrupting agriculture and humanitarian aid. Children are particularly affected, with an estimated 825,000 under five expected to suffer from severe acute malnutrition in 2026. The situation is exacerbated by the collapse of basic services and a lack of funding for humanitarian efforts.
key_takeaways: Over 19.5 million people in Sudan are experiencing crisis levels of food insecurity.
More than 5 million people are facing emergency levels of hunger.
Children are disproportionately affected, with 825,000 expected to suffer from severe acute malnutrition in 2026.
The conflict has uprooted nearly 9 million people and disrupted agriculture.
Only 20% of the 2026 Humanitarian Needs and Response Plan has been funded.
Urgent international funding and expanded humanitarian access are needed to prevent further deterioration.
document.why_it_matters: The situation in Sudan highlights the urgent need for international attention and support to address the humanitarian crisis exacerbated by ongoing conflict, economic collapse, and severe food shortages. The health and survival of millions, especially vulnerable children, are at stake.

claims.claim_text: Over 19.5 million people in Sudan are experiencing crisis levels of food insecurity.
claims.evidence_text: According to the assessment, over 19.5 million people – around two out of every five Sudanese – are experiencing crisis levels of food insecurity or worse.

claims.claim_text: Children are particularly affected by the hunger crisis.
claims.evidence_text: An estimated 825,000 children under five are expected to suffer from severe acute malnutrition in 2026.

claims.claim_text: Only 20% of Sudan's 2026 Humanitarian Needs and Response Plan has been funded.
claims.evidence_text: Only 20 per cent of Sudan’s 2026 Humanitarian Needs and Response Plan had been funded by April.

entities.name: UN World Food Programme
entities.description: A UN agency that provides food assistance and addresses hunger.
entities.evidence_text: The warning came in a joint alert issued by the UN World Food Programme (WFP), the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) and UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF).

entities.name: Food and Agriculture Organization
entities.description: A specialized agency of the United Nations that leads international efforts to defeat hunger.
entities.evidence_text: The warning came in a joint alert issued by the UN World Food Programme (WFP), the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) and UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF).

entities.name: UN Children's Fund
entities.description: A UN agency responsible for providing humanitarian and developmental aid to children.
entities.evidence_text: The warning came in a joint alert issued by the UN World Food Programme (WFP), the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) and UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF).

entities.name: UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs
entities.description: A UN body responsible for coordinating humanitarian response efforts.
entities.evidence_text: Photographer Giles Clarke, who recently travelled to eastern Sudan with support from the UN relief coordination office, OCHA.

events.evidence_text: Sudan has been engulfed in brutal conflict since April 2023, when fighting erupted between the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF).

events.event: Children admitted for treatment for severe acute malnutrition
events.evidence_text: Between January and March alone, nearly 100,000 children were admitted for treatment for severe acute malnutrition, according to the agencies.

metrics.name: People facing crisis levels of food insecurity
metrics.evidence_text: According to the assessment, over 19.5 million people – around two out of every five Sudanese – are experiencing crisis levels of food insecurity or worse.

metrics.name: Children under five suffering from severe acute malnutrition
metrics.evidence_text: An estimated 825,000 children under five are expected to suffer from severe acute malnutrition in 2026.

metrics.name: Funding of Humanitarian Needs and Response Plan
metrics.evidence_text: Only 20 per cent of Sudan’s 2026 Humanitarian Needs and Response Plan had been funded by April.

citations.title: Sudan hunger crisis deepens as UN warns millions face acute food shortages

source_credibility.reason: The source is a UN news outlet, providing official information from recognized international organizations.
